original_text: The United Nations brokers a ceasefire agreement in the Yemen conflict.
identified_property: comprehensiveness and durability of the ceasefire agreement

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: The United Nations brokers a narrow, temporary pause in hostilities in the Yemen conflict, limited to a single front and lasting only 72 hours with no monitoring provisions.

variations.label: low
variations.text: The United Nations brokers a short-term ceasefire agreement in the Yemen conflict, covering major urban areas for 30 days but lacking enforcement mechanisms or humanitarian access provisions.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: The United Nations brokers a partial ceasefire agreement in the Yemen conflict, covering most active fronts for several months but without provisions for prisoner exchanges or humanitarian aid corridors.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: The United Nations brokers a ceasefire agreement in the Yemen conflict.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: The United Nations brokers a nationwide ceasefire agreement in the Yemen conflict that includes humanitarian aid corridors, prisoner exchanges, and international monitoring mechanisms with a renewable six-month timeline.

variations.label: high
variations.text: The United Nations brokers a comprehensive and indefinite ceasefire agreement in the Yemen conflict that includes robust international monitoring, humanitarian access, demilitarized zones, prisoner exchanges, and a binding framework for political negotiations toward a permanent settlement.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: The United Nations brokers a permanent, fully comprehensive ceasefire agreement in the Yemen conflict that includes international enforcement guarantees, complete humanitarian access, disarmament provisions, power-sharing arrangements, and a binding roadmap to a durable peace with reconstruction commitments from the international community.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases the comprehensiveness (scope of provisions covered) and durability (temporal extent and enforcement strength) of the ceasefire. Tier 1 is a minimal 72-hour pause on one front; Tier 7 is a permanent, fully enforceable peace framework with reconstruction. All tiers describe the same core scenario of UN-brokered conflict resolution in Yemen.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a permanent, fully comprehensive ceasefire with enforcement guarantees, disarmament, power-sharing, and reconstruction commitments represents the most durable and complete resolution to the conflict, maximizing humanitarian benefit and long-term stability.
